title: Fix for overwriting LD_PRELOAD if it contains libscalene.so and other values
body:
Summary:
- The Linux LD_PRELOAD handling in Scalene can overwrite an existing LD_PRELOAD value when setup_preload runs multiple times. If LD_PRELOAD already contains libscalene.so (perhaps from a previous invocation), the code previously replaces it with just libscalene.so, thereby discarding other preloaded libraries.

What’s changing:
- Preserve and merge with any existing LD_PRELOAD content instead of overwriting it.
- Ensure libscalene.so is present in LD_PRELOAD without removing other entries, even if setup_preload is invoked multiple times.

Rationale:
- Idempotent behavior is essential when setup_preload can be called more than once. The new logic keeps all existing LD_PRELOAD entries intact and guarantees that libscalene.so is included exactly once.

Impact:
- Minor change in the environment setup path for Linux memory profiling. No external API changes. Should fix scenarios where LD_PRELOAD values get accidentally overwritten.

Tests to consider:
- Run profiling multiple times in a single process and verify LD_PRELOAD composition remains correct (libscalene.so present, no lost entries).
- Verify that LD_LIBRARY_PATH handling and PYTHONMALLOC behavior remain unaffected.

step_plan.summary: Fix for overwriting LD_PRELOAD by merging with existing content so repeated setup_preload invocations preserve other preloaded libraries and ensure libscalene.so is present without duplicates.

steps.operation: Refactor Linux LD_PRELOAD merging to preserve and merge with existing LD_PRELOAD
steps.reason: Currently setup_preload overwrites LD_PRELOAD, causing loss of other preloaded libraries; merge safely to ensure idempotence and include libscalene.so
steps.side_effects: Tests will need to cover idempotence across multiple invocations; potential minor changes to environment variable composition; no external API changes; may affect external processes relying on LD_PRELOAD ordering, but logic should preserve other entries and add libscalene.so when missing.
